title: The Unopened Envelope
hook: I hated my father for twenty years for his silence, until I opened his closet.
setup: He never replied to my childhood letters. I assumed he chose a life without me.
escalation: I found a dusty shoebox tucked behind his winter coats. Inside were my letters, all opened, and his unmailed replies.
climax: His letters revealed my mother’s criminal past, and his terrifying need to keep me hidden from her reach.
ending: He didn't abandon me; he sacrificed our relationship to keep me alive.
colorMood: desaturated greys and warm amber light
visualMoments: Close up of woman pulling a shoebox from a dark shelf.
Quick cuts of handwritten letters spilling onto a wooden floor.
Finger tracing ink on an old, unopened envelope.
Extreme close up of the narrator's face widening in realization.
The empty shoebox sitting alone in the dim light.
